title: ALPR WA 2026: H: All ALPR Scan Records (Kent Police Department)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: ALPR WA 2026: H: All ALPR Scan Records (Kent Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

## PRESERVATION DEMAND ##

Pursuant to O'Neill v. City of Shoreline, 170 Wn.2d 138 (2010), and WAC 44-14-03005, your agency's obligation to preserve all records responsive to this request attaches upon receipt.

Please immediately:

1. Suspend any automated deletion, overwrite, or purge process that would destroy records falling within the scope of this request, whether operating on your agency's own infrastructure or on vendor-managed infrastructure over which your agency holds a contractual right of access or retrieval.

2. Issue a written instruction to your ALPR vendor(s) directing them to halt any automated deletion of records within the scope of this request pending its resolution.

## VENDOR-HELD RECORDS ##

In Rodriguez v. City of Stanwood & Sedro-Woolley (Skagit County Superior Court, No. 22-2-00477-29, Nov. 6, 2025), the court held that ALPR data held by a third-party SaaS vendor on behalf of a government agency is a public record subject to PRA disclosure, and that vendor custody does not relieve the agency of its production obligation. That ruling is currently on appeal to the Washington Court of Appeals, Division 1; the appeal does not stay the disclosure obligation.  Each request below that encompasses vendor-held records is made on this basis. Please retrieve and produce all responsive records held by your ALPR vendor(s) on your agency's behalf.

## ELECTRONIC REPRODUCTION AND THE DUTY TO PRODUCE ##

I seek existing records only. I understand that no agency is required to create a new record in response to a PRA request. However, producing a screenshot or other faithful reproduction of an existing electronic display is not creating a new record — it is reproducing an existing one, in the same manner as photocopying or scanning a paper document. Where a requested record exists only as a display within a software interface, a screenshot or faithful reproduction of that display, or an export of that display to any available file format (including CSV, PDF, or JSON), is an acceptable and required form of production.

## FEE REQUEST ##

I am requesting these records for research and public interest purposes, submitted through MuckRock, a nonprofit journalism platform. I request that your agency consider waiving any fees given that this request is submitted for research and public interest purposes and that electronic production of the requested records should entail minimal or no actual cost under RCW 42.56.120. For any anticipated costs, please notify me before incurring them so that I may prioritize or narrow the request. This notification requirement should not be construed as authorization to delay production of records for which no fee will be incurred pending resolution of fees for other records in this request

## RECORDS REQUESTED ##

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All existing ALPR scan records (e.g., stored in your agency's ALPR system or any system used to store, process, or query ALPR data, such as Axon Evidence.com, Flock Safety portal, or Motorola/Vigilant), including all metadata fields associated with each record as captured and stored by the system. This includes but is not limited to: license plate number, timestamp, GPS coordinates, camera or device identifier, lane identifier, direction of travel, confidence score, vehicle make, vehicle model, vehicle color, image file metadata, and any field capturing information about vehicle occupants or associated individuals.

2. All system audit logs associated with the records in item 1, as natively maintained in your agency's systems or your vendor's systems, reflecting every query performed against your agency's ALPR system, including for each query: the date and time, the user identifier or badge number of the querying user, the Originating Agency Identifier (ORI) of the querying agency, and the entry in the "reason for search," "justification," "purpose," or equivalent field. Where no justification was entered for a query, the log entry reflecting that absence is itself a responsive record and should be produced.

To facilitate partial rolling production while the full dataset is being compiled, please prioritize production of records associated with the following specific license plates. Prioritizing these plates does not limit or waive any part of this request:

	Seattle Police Department specialized and long-service fleet vehicles
	(from City of Seattle fleet records; plates may have been re-registered
	but vehicles are believed to remain in service):
	c. 50816D  — Surveillance trailer (Skywatch Sentinel, 2010)
	d. 50887D  — Hybrid command center (2007, est. replacement 2031)
	e. 54180D  — Police armored vehicle (2011, est. replacement 2031)
	f. 43500D  — Bomb squad trailer (2005)
	g. 26073D  — Bomb containment vessel trailer (1997)
	h. 41461D  — Communications van (2005)
	i. 44372D  — Command vehicle (2005)
	j. 54148D  — Riot fencing trailer (2011)
	k. 54362D  — CSI van with generator (2012)
	l. 50295D  — SWAT cutaway van (2008)
	m. 54117D  — CSI van (2011)
	n. 24208D  — Weapons vehicle (1987)
	
	Washington State fleet vehicles (by plate pattern):
	o. All plates with the prefix or suffix "WSP"
	p. All plates with the prefix "XMT"
	
	Other:
	a. BCH-478
	b. 826-ZVA

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Phil Mocek

The records are provided to you along with an exemption log that identifies the specific exemption authorizing the withholding/redacting, and a brief explanation of how the exemption applies to the record withheld, in whole or in part.

You may make your payment by check, payable to the “Kent City Treasurer,” or by credit card. Credit card payments must be made in person or over the phone to City of Kent customer service at 253-856-5201; please note a fee may be added to credit or debit card payments.  If mailing payment, please address your envelope to my attention in the City Clerk’s Office. Payment must be received within 30 calendar days from the date of this letter. If payment is not received within 30 calendar days, the City will close your request. In accordance with the City's Public Records Request Policy located on the City's website; copied records, or records gathered for inspection, will be available to the requester for 30 calendar days from the date of the City's notification to the requester that the records are available.  If a requester fails to retrieve or inspect the responsive records within that 30-day period, the records request will be deemed abandoned, the records will be returned to the originating department, and the City will close the request. If the records were produced as an installment response under section 1.5.3.4.C, the requester's failure to retrieve or inspect an installment will result in the City discontinuing its search for any remaining records, closing the entire records request. We are continuing to process responsive records for Installment #2, once the deposit is paid t he City currently estimates it will be able to provide responsive installment #2 records, either on an installment basis within 30 business days of receipt of the deposit. If you have any questions, please contact the City Clerk’s Office at 253-856-5725. 
Thank you. Sincerely, Tiffany Alcorn Tiffany Alcorn CPRO - Lead Public Disclosure Administrator 
City of Kent Administration 
Arthur “Pat” Fitzpatrick, Chief Administrative Officer

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #W078473-031226
communications.communication:
Hi Tiffany and Pat.

This is a heads-up that I plan to stop by the City Clerk's Office during business hours (Monday–Friday, 8:00–5:00) to inspect the records identified as Installment #1 in your April 1 message.  I'll bring a USB drive so I can copy what I need without requiring anything from your staff--no printing, no emailing, no fee.

Since inspection is free under RCW 42.56.120(1), the $90.50 copying fee from your April 1 letter won't apply to what I inspect in person. And since no appointment is needed under RCW 42.56.080, I'm just flagging this in advance so the records can be pulled and ready before I arrive rather than having anyone scramble when I show up.

This notice also keeps the request open while the separate questions about Installment #2's fee structure and production format get sorted out--I will send another message addressing those problems shortly after this one.

Thanks for your help with this.

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #W078473-031226
communications.communication:
RE: Public Records Request — ALPR Scan Records; Fee and Format Dispute

Dear City of Kent Public Records Officer,

This responds to the City's April 1, 2026 correspondence. The requester disputes the fee methodology applied to both installments, objects to the production format, and renews the fee waiver request the City's April 1 letter did not address.

## I. The Fee Methodology Must Reflect Actual Cost ##

RCW 42.56.120(2)(b)(iii) authorizes a fee of $0.05 per four electronic files "uploaded to an email, cloud storage service, or other electronic delivery system." This fee is calculated per file uploaded — not per row of data within a file, and not per image associated with a database record. The validity of the City's fee calculation therefore depends entirely on how many discrete files are actually uploaded, which depends on the production format addressed in Section II.

The requester requests that the City provide in writing: (1) whether it has conducted a cost study pursuant to RCW 42.56.120(2)(b) or is applying the statutory default schedule; (2) if applying the statutory default, the published declaration required by WAC 44-14-07001 stating why calculating actual costs would be unduly burdensome; and (3) identification of the specific policy or mayoral directive authorizing the fee schedule applied. Web research has not identified any such published declaration by the City of Kent. Without it, the City lacks the required legal predicate for applying the statutory default rate, and the fee demand is accordingly unsupported. The requester requests production of that declaration if it exists.

## II. Producing a Structured Database as 500,000 Discrete Image Files Is Inconsistent with the City's Obligations Under RCW 42.56.100 and RCW 42.56.120 ##

The request sought all ALPR scan records and associated metadata, and explicitly requested production in electronic database format, including CSV, PDF, or JSON export. ALPR scan records are generated and stored as structured data within the Flock Safety platform — a cloud-based database system. Each scan record is a database entry comprising structured metadata fields: timestamp, GPS coordinates, plate number, camera identifier, confidence score, and associated image reference. The records do not exist natively as 500,000 discrete, independent image files; they exist as a structured dataset, the native export format of which is a single tabular data file such as a CSV.

Flock Safety provides authorized agency administrators a bulk export function as a standard platform feature. Producing a single CSV export of the responsive dataset requires no custom programming, no specialized IT expertise, and no per-file upload process. The actual cost to the City of transmitting a single CSV file is de minimis.

RCW 42.56.100 requires agencies to provide "the fullest assistance" to requesters. RCW 42.56.120 requires that "[w]hen calculating any fees authorized under this section, an agency shall use the most reasonable cost-efficient method available to the agency as part of its normal operations." The native bulk CSV export available through the Flock Safety administrative interface is both more reasonable and more cost-efficient than extracting 500,000 individual image files. Recharacterizing a structured database as 500,000 discrete files to trigger a per-file fee — where a single-file export is available, was explicitly requested, and requires no custom programming — is not consistent with either provision.

The requester requests that the City produce the responsive records as a single native database export (CSV, JSON, or equivalent) from the Flock Safety system and recalculate any applicable fee on that basis. Under this methodology, the fee for installment #2 would be $0.05 for one file, not $6,250.00 for 500,000 files.

## III. The Fee Waiver Request Has Not Been Addressed ##

The original request expressly requested fee waiver, citing research and public interest purposes, the nonprofit nature of the MuckRock platform through which the request was submitted, and the minimal actual cost of electronic production under RCW 42.56.120. The City's April 1 letter does not address this request. The requester renews it and requests a written response, including the specific basis for any denial.

## IV. Notice of Intent to Inspect ##

Pursuant to RCW 42.56.080, the requester provides notice of intent to inspect the records identified as responsive to installment #1 in person at the City Clerk's Office, 220 4th Avenue South, Kent, WA 98032, during published business hours (Monday through Friday, 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.). In-person inspection carries no charge under RCW 42.56.120(1); the $90.50 fee stated in the April 1 letter does not apply to records inspected in person. The requester will bring a USB drive for self-copying; no agency copying fee will be incurred for records copied using the requester's own equipment. This notice is provided so that the records may be made ready before arrival. This notice also stops the running of any abandonment period under the City's records policy pending resolution of the fee and format dispute regarding installment #2.

## V. Requested Actions ##

The requester requests that the City:

1. Provide in writing: (a) whether it has conducted a cost study under RCW 42.56.120(2)(b) or is applying the statutory default; (b) if the latter, the published unduly-burdensome declaration required by WAC 44-14-07001; and (c) the specific policy or mayoral directive authorizing the fee schedule applied;
2. Produce installment #2 as a single native database export (CSV, JSON, or equivalent) from the Flock Safety system and recalculate the applicable fee accordingly; and
3. Provide a written response to the fee waiver request, including the basis for any denial.

The requester requests a response within five business days.

Cordially,
Phil Mocek

communications.subject: Other Public Records Updated :: W078473-031226
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
05/08/2026 Phil Mocek MuckRock News, DEPT MR206498 Boston, MA 02115 Re: Public Records Request, dated 3/12/2026 File #W078473-031226 for Other Public Records Matter – Installment # 1 - Records Available; Installment #2- Additional Time Needed to Respond Dear Phil Mocek:

"On 3/12/2026, you submitted a public records request to the City of Kent wherein you sought records related to automated license plate readers in the City of Kent. On 4/1/2026, the City indicated that the first installment of records was available and that copies could be obtained upon the City’s receipt of your fee payment.

 

As you may be aware, on 3/30/2026, Engrossed Substitute Senate Bill 6002 (“SB 6002”) was signed into law and became effective that same day. Section 5(5) of this bill provides that “[a]utomated license plate reader data is not subject to disclosure under the public records act, chapter 42.56 RCW … ” Automated license plate reader (“ALPR”) data is defined in Section 2(3) of this bill as “all data collected by automated license plate reader systems including, but not limited to, global positioning system coordinates, location, date and time, speed of travel, photograph, license plate number, automobile characteristics, or other identifying information.”

 

At the time of the City’s 4/1/2026 communication to you, the City had believed that the above referenced sections of SB 6002 were inapplicable to public records requests that had been received by a public agency before this new legislation took effect. However, in the time that has passed since, the City Attorney’s Office has advised that, consistent with Serv. Emps. Int'l Union Local 925 v. Dep't of Early Learning, 194 Wn.2d 546 (Wash. 2019) and Wash. Educ. Ass'n v. Dep't of Ret. Sys., 21 Wn. App. 2d 736 (Wash. Ct. App. 2022), those records that meet the definition of “ALPR data” are no longer subject to disclosure and must be withheld consistent with SB 6002, even for those public records requests that were received by an agency prior to that law taking effect. Under Washington State case law, certain newly created exemptions under the Public Records Act are to be assessed at the time the records are made available for public inspection or copying, as opposed to the date the public records request was originally made. As a result, those records you requested that meet the definition of “ALPR data” under SB 6002 are no longer able to be inspected or released to you in response to your public records request.

 

The City of Kent has completed an updated Installment #1 of records relating to the above-referenced public records request. As noted below, this Installment #1 is being provided consistent with SB 6002 and any records it may exempt from disclosure or that may now require additional redaction.

 

As indicated above, ALPR data is not subject to disclosure pursuant to Section 5(5) of SB 6002. The City will therefore be withholding records that include ALPR photos, identifying data collected by ALPR systems, and other records included in the statutory definition of “ALPR data.” The records that are being withheld as part of this Installment #1 are noted in the accompanying exemption log. An additional log will accompany Installment #2 where exempt records are withheld.

 

Some records are being redacted in accordance with the Public Records Act and may include redactions where an exempt portion of the record was “blacked out” or a record withheld in its entirety. The records are provided to you along with an exemption log that identifies the specific exemption authorizing the withholding/redacting, and a brief explanation of how the exemption applies to the record withheld, in whole or in part.

 

There is no applicable fee for the records being provided under Installment #1 due to the size of the files. While we believe your inquiries regarding the City’s fee structure sent on 5/1 and 5/3 are now moot, the information you requested can be found and accessed at this link.

 

The records included within Installment #1 are available at the City’s on-line Public Records Center.  You may access the records by following the below link and instructions:

1. Visit KentWA.gov/PRR

2. Click on "My Request Center"

3. Click on "View My Requests"

4. Login to your account, click "Submit"

5. Click on the File # for this request.

6. The electronic records are available in the "Files" window.


In accordance with the City's Public Records Request Policy located on the City's website; copied records, or records gathered for inspection, will be available to the requester for 30 calendar days from the date of the City's notification to the requester that the records are available.  If a requester fails to retrieve or inspect the responsive records within that 30-day period, the records request will be deemed abandoned, the records will be returned to the originating department, and the City will close the request. If the records were produced as an installment response under section 1.5.3.4.C, the requester's failure to retrieve or inspect an installment will result in the City discontinuing its search for any remaining records, closing the entire records request.

 

You had previously indicated that you desired to inspect these records in person to avoid fees. While this updated installment does not have any applicable fees, please contact our office at 253-865-5725 to schedule an appointment for your inspection if you would still wish to inspect records in person. Due to cybersecurity concerns, we cannot allow requesters to interface with City devices via their own USB devices. We are happy to discuss other methods of providing copies of records prior to any scheduled inspection.

 

We are continuing to process responsive records for Installment #2. The City currently estimates it will be able to provide responsive records, either on an installment basis or in total by 05/21/2026. 


If you are not able to access the records at the Public Records Center, or if you have any questions, please contact the City Clerk’s Office at 253-856-5725.

 

Thank you.

Sincerely,

Tiffany Alcorn

Tiffany Alcorn

CPRO - Lead Public Disclosure Administrator


City of Kent Administration
Arthur “Pat” Fitzpatrick, Chief Administrative Officer"

title: Fusus manuals, audit logs, and metadata

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Fusus manuals, audit logs, and metadata
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) User manuals, handbooks, or comparable instructional documents provided to the department by Fusus regarding the following Fusus products and services:
- FususONE (https://www.fusus.com/rtc3-products/fususone)
- FususCORE (https://www.fusus.com/rtc3-products/fususcore)
- FususREGISTRY (https://www.fusus.com/rtc3-products/fususregistry)
- Fusus AI Video Analytics (https://www.fusus.com/rtc3-products/fusus-ai)
- Fusus Intelligent AI Alerts (https://www.fusus.com/rtc3-products/fusus-ai)

2) Audit logs/audit trails documenting each time a department account has accessed live or recorded video footage through the Fusus platforms from 5/20/2025 until the date this request is processed. To assist with your search, I've attached an example of such an audit log from another department that uses Fusus.  This data should be stored in a database and so I ask that you provide it to me as a  .csv, .xls, or other database filetype.

3) Metadata for each Intelligent AI Alert generated by the department's Fusus system from 5/20/2025 until the date this request is processed. Fusus Intelligent AI Alerts automatically notify authorized users when Fusus AI-connected cameras detect pre-defined events (e.g. a fight) or profiles (e.g. a vehicle of interest). Please note that I am not requesting the video footage or any personally identifying information associated with the alert, but rather the metadata. Metadata may include, but is not limited to: the type of alert, the date and time of the alert, who received the alert, locations associated with the alert, and algorithmic confidence scores associated with the alert. This metadata should be stored in a database and so I ask that you provide it to me as a  .csv, .xls, or other database filetype.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

Dear Rose Terse, 
Your records have been released and are available on the Public Records Request Center to download. This is the 2nd and final installment of records. This includes the fusus user manual. Be advised, this is a web-based user manual that has no PDF'd version. Therefore all of these are screen grabs and some of the pages exist on multiple pages. This completes the request. 
To download your records, please: 
 
  * Navigate to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * Select 'My Records Request Center'. 
  * Select 'View My Requests'. You may be asked to login. 
  * Select this request: P227619-060125. 
  * Select the 'View Files' button. 
  * You can download records individually or all at the same time by clicking 'Download All'. 
  *  Please save your records as you may only download them from the system three times. If you do not download your records within 30 days of request closure, the link to your records will expire. 
If you believe you have received privileged or confidential records in error, please notify the City of Seattle immediately, through the Public Records Request Center or by email. Privileged or confidential records provided or received in error should not be reviewed, used, disseminated, distributed, or copied, and should be destroyed immediately. Thank you in advance for your cooperation. 
SPD will take no further action on this request. If you have questions regarding the processing of this request or would like to initiate an administrative appeal regarding the response to your request, please do so within 10 business days. You may also seek judicial review of our response to your request for a period of one year, beginning as of the date of this message, consistent with RCW 42.56.550. 
Please include your name and address, a copy of any redacted files, and a copy of this letter together with a brief statement identifying the basis of the appeal. 
Please submit your appeal via GovQA, the mail, or deliver your appeal to: 
Seattle Police Department 
Attn: Legal Section 
610 Fifth Avenue 
P.O. Box 34986 
Seattle, WA 98124-4986 
 
If you have any questions, you may send a message using the Police Records Request Center or call the Public Disclosure Desk at 206-684-5481. 
 
Sincerely, 
Jackson Colcord 
Seattle Police Department 
Legal Unit 
 
SPD receives over 13,000 public records requests annually and is processing between 2,500 to 3,000 open requests at any given time. This may result in a slower than anticipated response to your request. Thank you for your 
patience. 
 
As a reminder you can login at the Police Records Request Center where you can access Police online information, manage your profile, submit record requests, track your request status, receive and send messages, make payments, view frequently asked questions, and download your records. 
 Please add seattle@mycusthelp.net to your E-mail contacts/address book to ensure delivery of the record center E-mails to your Inbox. (mailto:seattle@mycusthelp.net)

title: Internal Complaints (2010 - Present) (Yakima County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Internal Complaints (2010 - Present) (Yakima County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

For the period from January 1, 2010 to the date this request was filed, please provide a spreadsheet of all complaints against officers in CSV or Excel format with only the following columns: Investigation/Complaint No., Incident Type, Occurred Date, Complaint Reported On Date, Employee Badge Number, Employee First Name, Employee Last Name, Allegation, Allegation: Policy/Directive Violated, Allegation: Finding, Disposition, Discipline Imposed.

Please provide all complaints, regardless of severity,in cluding any complaints that may not have been classified for full investigation and were referred to an employee's supervisor for counseling or retraining as well as complaints that were investigated by an employee's command instead of the normal internal affairs process. I would ask that you fulfill my request in a single installment in one spreadsheet instead of splitting it up into multiple annual spreadsheets if possible.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

title: Faculty Search Records Request

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Faculty Search Records Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140), I hereby request the following public records related to the faculty recruitment and hiring process for the 2026-2027 (current) academic cycle within the Grainger College of Engineering.

This request includes records for tenure-track, non-tenure track, teaching, and Specialized Faculty positions. The specific list of job openings/requisition ID include, but are not limited to:
1. Department of Aerospace Engineering - Open Rank Faculty Search / Requisition ID 1032680 Posted on 10/8/2025
2. Department of Industrial & Enterprise Systems Engineering- Open Rank Faculty Search / Requisition ID 1032898 Posted on 10/13/2025
3. Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering – Open Rank Faculty Search / Requisition ID 1033201 Posted on 11/11/2025
4. Department of Bioengineering - Teaching Faculty (Open Rank) / Requisition ID 1034104 Posted on 12/19/2025
5. The Grainger College of Engineering: Professor - Siebel School of Computing and Data Science / Requisition ID 1035082 Posted on 3/20/2026

Requested Records:

Please note that the request is for any and all interviews which may have occurred between January 1, 2026 and April 25, 2026.

1. Interview Recordings: Copies of any and all video or audio recordings of candidate screening interviews, specifically for candidates who provided written consent for such recordings to be made and maintained, as outlined in the UIUC Recruitment and Hiring Guidelines (Effective January 1, 2026).

2. Faculty Candidate Seminar recordings: Copies of any and all video or audio recordings of faculty candidate seminars, specifically for candidates who provided written consent for such recordings to be made and maintained, as outlined in the UIUC Recruitment and Hiring Guidelines (Effective January 1, 2026), even if the Faculty Candidate Seminar is publically advertised as a Departmental Seminar due to confidentiality requests from the candidate.

3. Interview Notes: Copies of all formal interview notes taken by search committee members (excluding personal "memory aides" not shared with the committee, as defined by Illinois law).

This request specifically EXCLUDES:

1. Evaluation Documents: Search committee evaluation forms, rubrics, ranking sheets, and final reports regarding the candidates for the specified positions.
2. Correspondence: Emails, memoranda, and internal communications regarding the selection, ranking, and final recommendations for these hires.
3. Meeting minutes of the Faculty Hiring Committee.


Privacy and Redactions:

Consistent with Section 7(1)(b) and 7(1)(c) of the Act, I request that you redact sensitive personal information such as Social Security Numbers (SSN), home addresses, personal phone numbers, and any other "unique identifiers" as defined by FOIA.

If you determine that any specific record or portion of a record is exempt from disclosure (e.g., the names of unsuccessful candidates who did not consent), please provide a written justification for each redaction or denial, citing the specific FOIA exemption, and release the remaining non-exempt portions of the records.

Format of Records:
I request that these records be provided in their original electronic format (e.g., PDF, MP4, or Excel) via email or a secure file-sharing link.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within the five (5) business days required by the Act.

Sincerely,

Joseph Sentien Sief

title: Community ALPR Audit 2 (McLean County Sheriff)

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Community ALPR Audit 2 (McLean County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

1. Ideological/Identity Based Search Records

All Flock search records (including Organization Audit logs with purpose fields) where the stated reason, tag, or description references any protest, demonstration, march, rally, or political/social justice event or organization or refers to a protected characteristic such as race, gender, nationality, religion, disability, etc. including slang and unredacted slurs.

2. Misuse and Discipline Records

All internal affairs complaints, internal notes, investigative reports, disciplinary records, or personnel actions related to potential misuse of Flock Safety systems (including stalking, personal use, or unauthorized access) from installation to present.

3. False Positives and Wrongful Stops

All records documenting false-positive identifications, misidentifications, erroneous “hot list” alerts, or resulting wrongful stops/detentions tied to Flock data, including internal complaints, incident reports, or legal claims.

4. Flock Raven/Audio Detection Deployment

All records related to any Flock Safety Raven acoustic sensors or audio detection capabilities operated by this agency, including purchase orders, installation records, location lists, and any policies describing audio capture, storage, access, and retention.

5. Audio Recording Legal Analysis

All internal or external legal opinions, memoranda, or analyses regarding whether Flock Raven or similar audio-capable devices comply with Illinois eavesdropping law (720 ILCS 5/14-2), including assessments of potential recording inside vehicles, homes, or locations with a reasonable expectation of privacy.

6. AI Features Usage (FreeForm)

All records describing or referencing the use of Flock’s FreeForm search feature or any AI-assisted search tool by this agency, including training materials, usage policies, internal audits, or guidance on when/how such features may be employed.

7. Warrant vs. Warrantless Search Policy

All written policies, procedures, or guidance addressing whether officers must obtain a warrant or court order before accessing Flock data or conducting Flock searches, including any limitations on historical data searches.

8. External Agency Data Sharing Details

Beyond the basic authorized agencies list, please provide any available details about:
- Specific types of data shared with each external agency
- Frequency or volume of sharing with major recipients (particularly federal agencies)
- Any memoranda of understanding or data-sharing agreements governing external access

9. Internal Search Purpose Transparency

The Flock Organization Audit report showing all internal searches with the full, unredacted “purpose” or “reason” field for each search, in machine-readable format (CSV/spreadsheet), to assess patterns of use and potential discriminatory targeting.

10. Data Retention and Deletion Policies

All records describing this agency’s data retention and deletion practices for Flock Safety data, including:
- Written policies on how long Flock data is retained
- Procedures for deleting or purging data
- Any records of incidents where data was retained beyond stated periods or not properly deleted
- Communications with Flock Safety regarding data retention capabilities or limitations
- Audits or a lack thereof which verify data is actually deleted and shared only as per stated policy

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brad Bell

The Flock Organization Audit report showing all internal searches with the full, unredacted “purpose” or “reason” field for each search, in machine-readable format (CSV/spreadsheet), to assess patterns of use and potential discriminatory targeting. 

10. Data Retention and Deletion Policies 

All records describing this agency’s data retention and deletion practices for Flock Safety data, including:

- Audits or a lack thereof which verify data is actually deleted and shared only as per stated policy 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Please reply to this message and clarify your request by specifying dates in which you are requesting search records.

You have the right to appeal a closed request (i.e., a denial) by a) submitting a written request for review within 60 days of the denial to the Public Access Counselor, Office of the Illinois Attorney General, Public Access Bureau, 500 S. 2nd Street, Springfield, IL 62701 (phone number 1-877-299-3642) or b) filing suit for injunctive or declaratory relief in the McLean County Circuit Court.

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request #W012216-042826
communications.communication:
Thank you for your response dated April 29, 2026. I note that your letter states my request is “too broad as written” but does not specify which particular items you find overbroad, explain why compliance with any item would be unduly burdensome, or identify the specific burden (e.g., volume of records, review time) as required under 5 ILCS 140/3(g).

In the interest of resolving this promptly and in good faith, I offer the following clarifications and date ranges:

For items 1, 2, 3, 8, and 9 (which request search records, discipline logs, false-positive reports, data-sharing details, and audit logs) let us specify the request from the date of Flock Safety system installation through April 30, 2026.

For items 4, 5, 6, 7, and 10, these request current and recently superseded written guidance documents. Please provide all versions of responsive documents currently in effect or that have been superseded since the Flock system’s installation.

If you would identify which item(s) you contend are burdensome, explain the specific burden, and propose a narrowing, we may confer further as the statute requires.

I look forward to your specific response so we may proceed. Per 5 ILCS 140/3(b), I anticipate a response within 5 business days.

RESPONSE TO REQUEST FOR PUBLIC RECORDS 
To: Brad Bell 
Email: 209557-07507955@requests.muckrock.com 
Address: MuckRock News, DEPT MR209557 263 Huntington Ave Boston MA 02115 
From FOIA Officer: Katlin Crow 
Department: COUNTY SHERIFF 
 
Response Date: 05/08/2026 RE: Your Sheriff FOIA request W012216-042826 dated 4/29/2026 
For the following records: 
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

1. Ideological/Identity Based Search Records 

All Flock search records (including Organization Audit logs with purpose fields) where the stated reason, tag, or description references any protest, demonstration, march, rally, or political/social justice event or organization or refers to a protected characteristic such as race, gender, nationality, religion, disability, etc. including slang and unredacted slurs. 

2. Misuse and Discipline Records 

All internal affairs complaints, internal notes, investigative reports, disciplinary records, or personnel actions related to potential misuse of Flock Safety systems (including stalking, personal use, or unauthorized access) from installation to present. 

3. False Positives and Wrongful Stops 

All records documenting false-positive identifications, misidentifications, erroneous “hot list” alerts, or resulting wrongful stops/detentions tied to Flock data, including internal complaints, incident reports, or legal claims. 

4. Flock Raven/Audio Detection Deployment 

All records related to any Flock Safety Raven acoustic sensors or audio detection capabilities operated by this agency, including purchase orders, installation records, location lists, and any policies describing audio capture, storage, access, and retention. 

5. Audio Recording Legal Analysis 

All internal or external legal opinions, memoranda, or analyses regarding whether Flock Raven or similar audio-capable devices comply with Illinois eavesdropping law (720 ILCS 5/14-2), including assessments of potential recording inside vehicles, homes, or locations with a reasonable expectation of privacy. 

6. AI Features Usage (FreeForm) 

All records describing or referencing the use of Flock’s FreeForm search feature or any AI-assisted search tool by this agency, including training materials, usage policies, internal audits, or guidance on when/how such features may be employed. 

7. Warrant vs. Warrantless Search Policy 

All written policies, procedures, or guidance addressing whether officers must obtain a warrant or court order before accessing Flock data or conducting Flock searches, including any limitations on historical data searches. 

8. External Agency Data Sharing Details 

Beyond the basic authorized agencies list, please provide any available details about: 
- Specific types of data shared with each external agency 
- Frequency or volume of sharing with major recipients (particularly federal agencies) 
- Any memoranda of understanding or data-sharing agreements governing external access 

9. Internal Search Purpose Transparency 

The Flock Organization Audit report showing all internal searches with the full, unredacted “purpose” or “reason” field for each search, in machine-readable format (CSV/spreadsheet), to assess patterns of use and potential discriminatory targeting. 

10. Data Retention and Deletion Policies 

All records describing this agency’s data retention and deletion practices for Flock Safety data, including: 
- Written policies on how long Flock data is retained 
- Procedures for deleting or purging data 
- Any records of incidents where data was retained beyond stated periods or not properly deleted 
- Communications with Flock Safety regarding data retention capabilities or limitations 
- Audits or a lack thereof which verify data is actually deleted and shared only as per stated policy 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Brad Bell 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2274002/

communications.subject: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: Wayne County PA Courthouse (Openrecords@waynecountypa.gov)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All current, fully executed Collective Bargaining Agreements/ Union Contracts between
Wayne County and any all-labor unions or bargaining units representing county employees.
This request includes, but is not limited to, contracts covering:
Court-Related Employees (e.g., Probation and Parole, Domestic Relations)Sheriffs/ Jail Deputies
Human Services/ Children and Youth Services
Any general county employee bargaining units or clerical units.
Any active MOUs or MOAs or formal contract extensions for any of the aforementioned
bargaining units currently operating under an expired contract while renegotiations are
pending.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

George Klein

communications.subject: RE: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: Wayne County PA Courthouse (Openrecords@waynecountypa.gov)
communications.communication:
Mr. Klein,

I am in receipt of your Right To Know Request received via email on May 6, 2026.
Your request has been approved and all current union contracts are attached. We only have a Corrections Officer contract, a Prison Sgts contract, a County Detectives Contract and a Probation/Domestic Relations Contract. All are attached. These are the only responsive records we have to your request.


To appeal this response, you must file one in writing within 15 business days of its emailing date pursuant to Section 1101 of the Pennsylvania Right-to-Know Law. Appeals should be sent to the Office of Open Records, 333 Market St., Harrisburg, PA 17126. Or you may file an appeal through the OOR's online appeal portal<https://www.openrecords.pa.gov/Appeals/AppealForm.cfm> form.  Please be advised that this correspondence will serve to close this record with our office as permitted by law.



Sincerely,
Andrew


Andrew M. Seder
Chief Clerk of Wayne County/Open Records Officer
925 Court St.
Honesdale, PA 18431
570-253-5970, ext. 1301

title: WSDOT: Graffiti Reporting Website Form Submissions (Non-Personal Fields)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: WSDOT: Graffiti Reporting Website Form Submissions (Non-Personal Fields)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request access to records maintained by the Washington State Department of Transportation related to submissions made through the agency’s “Report Graffiti” web form. 

For guidance, this form can be found online here: https://wsdot.wa.gov/about/contacts/report-graffiti

Specifically, I am seeking records reflecting the non-personal data fields submitted through this form, including:

Date of sighting
Highway
Direction
Location (free-text field)
Description (free-text field)

Scope & Format:

Please provide records for the time period of March 1st 2022 to April 14th 2026. 
I prefer the data in a structured, machine-readable format (e.g., CSV, Excel, or JSON), if available.

Exclusions:
To avoid any privacy concerns, I am NOT requesting and do NOT wish to receive any personally identifiable information submitted via the form, including:

First name
Last name
Email address
Any other contact or identifying information associated with the reporting individual

If any portion of the requested data is exempt from disclosure, please provide the non-exempt portions and cite the specific exemption(s) applied.

If there are any questions about this request or if it can be refined to reduce burden or improve responsiveness, I would be happy to discuss.

Thank you for the Washington State Department of Transportation's commitment to transparency and for the work that the agency does each and every day.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

We have reviewed your request and require the following clarification before we can determine responsive records: 
We have tracking spreadsheets with the requested information dating back to January of 2024. Would this date range be sufficient for your request? The information prior to that was not tracked in spreadsheets and would need to be located using an electronic records search to find the individual emails that are generated when there is a submittal. Additionally, we do not track or maintain the date of sighting information, instead we track based on the date of submittal. We can provide this information in place of the date of sighting. Please let me know how you would like to proceed. 
A reply must be received on the items requiring clarification for us to move forward. If no response or confirmation is received, we will supply records for the portions (if any) of your request that did not require clarification and then close the request due to no response. 

You can reply to this email with your clarification or send a message by logging into our online Public Disclosure Request Center. 

Thank you for your attention. 

Sincerely, 

Rodney Treece 
Public Disclosure Coordinator 
Washington State Department of Transportation

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #P020305-041426
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Rodney Treece-

You wrote, "We have tracking spreadsheets with the requested information dating back to January of 2024. Would this date range be sufficient for your request? " 

Yes, absolutely. 

You also wrote, "we do not track or maintain the date of sighting information, instead we track based on the date of submittal. We can provide this information in place of the date of sighting." 

The submittal date is perfectly acceptable. 

 Thank you for the update. 

Robert Delaware

communications.communication:

Any settlements between the City of Detroit and Michael Kelly -- or companies associated with Michael Kelly -- that resulted from eminent domain lawsuits around the city's neighborhood solar initiative.

communications.subject: Fw: [EXTERNAL] Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Michael Kelly solar farm settlement
communications.communication:
This email serves as the City of Detroit’s response to the above-referenced matter. Your request was received at the City of Detroit Law Department Freedom of Information Act Section via facsimile or email on December 4, 2025. Because your request was received by electronic transmission, pursuant to Section 5(1) of the Michigan Freedom of Information Act (the “Act”), MCL 15.235(1); it is deemed to have been received at the Law Department on the next business day, December 5, 2025.

Pursuant to Section 5(2) of the Act, MCL 15.235(2), the City’s response is due within five (5) business days. However, due to the nature and the scope of your request and the volume of the requests received by the City, we are extending the City’s response deadline by ten (10) additional business days in accordance with Section 5(2) (d) of the Act, MCL 15.235(2) (d). Therefore, your request will be granted, denied, or granted in part and denied in part on or before December 30, 2025.

Your request is being handled by Amanda Kelly. If you have questions regarding your request, or if you did not provide an email address in your request, please forward it to Amanda Kelly at kellya@detroitmi.gov<mailto:kellya@detroitmi.gov> to provide you a response more readily than by regular mail or fax. Mail and fax are not preferred at this time since they both require in-office support. We thank you in advance for your understanding.

title: Safety National Complaints (Division of Financial Services, Insurance)

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Safety National Complaints (Division of Financial Services, Insurance)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting access to all records in your possession from January 1, 2014, through the present concerning complaints, investigations, or regulatory actions involving Safety National Casualty Corporation, including any affiliated entities such as Safety Specialty Insurance Company and Sedgwick Claims Management Services, Inc. 

Specifically, I request copies of all consumer complaints alleging claim denials, delays, bad faith practices, misrepresentation of coverage, or failure to defend or indemnify insureds; any related correspondence or internal assessments; all regulatory enforcement actions, including market conduct examinations, administrative penalties, consent orders, fines, stipulations, or notices of violation; and any internal memoranda, emails, audit findings, or documentation regarding systemic or repeated misconduct. I also seek any statistical reports, dashboards, or complaint trend summaries reflecting activity related to Safety National during this period.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Fecteau

communications.subject: New Message :: R000023-011125
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

RE: FOIL Records Request of January 11, 2025, Reference # R000023-011125 
Dear Matthew Fecteau, 
The NYS Department of Financial Services received a FOIL request from you on January 11, 2025. You requested: "Pursuant to applicable laws and regulations, I am requesting access to any and all records, documents, and information related to First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company that are pertinent to assessing corporate governance, intercompany relationships, and financial practices. This request covers the period from January 1, 2018, to the date this request is processed (attached is an outdated but relevant document). Specifically, I seek: 

Corporate Governance and Structure: 

Organizational charts detailing First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company’s relationship with its parent companies, including Reliance Standard Life Insurance, Delphi Financial Group, and Tokio Marine Holdings, and its affiliates. 
Board meeting minutes and resolutions involving First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company and its parent entities. 
Policies or communications governing decision-making processes, including approval of dividends or capital allocations. 
Financial Records: 

Records of intercompany loans, transfers, or payments involving First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company and its affiliates or parent companies. 
Financial statements, audits, or reports evidencing First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company’s capitalization, reserves, and financial independence. 
Documentation on dividend payments made by First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company to its parent companies, including justification and impact on surplus reserves. 
Operational Independence: 

Service agreements, cost-sharing agreements, or contracts between First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company and affiliated entities, such as claims management, IT services, and administrative support. 
Documents showing First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company’s reinsurance arrangements, including agreements with affiliated or unaffiliated reinsurers. 
Compliance and Oversight: 

Correspondence with regulatory agencies, including the New York State Department of Financial Services, regarding First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company’s compliance with insurance regulations and financial standards. 
Internal audit reports or external reviews addressing First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company’s operations, financial health, or governance. 
Shared Resources and Branding: 

Records or policies demonstrating shared resources, personnel, or branding between First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company and its parent companies or affiliates. 
Communications discussing First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company’s operational independence or its role within the broader Tokio Marine Group. 
If certain records are exempt from disclosure, please provide the non-exempt portions along with a detailed explanation of the exemptions applied. I am willing to pay reasonable fees associated with fulfilling this request, but I request notification in advance if costs exceed a reasonable amount. 

Please send responsive documents electronically to my email address if possible. I appreciate your attention to this matter and look forward to your response within the timeframe required by law." The New York State Department of Financial Services (DFS) requires additional time to respond to your request, assigned the above Reference Number, because we are still working on your request. 
DFS will endeavor to provide you the determination of your FOIL request by August 15, 2025, or sooner if possible. However, if DFS is unable to complete its review by that time, you will then be provided an update on the progress of your FOIL request. Your continued patience is greatly appreciated. 
Sincerely, Lisa Derrick 
Administrative Specialist 
NYS Department of Financial Services 
Office of General Counsel

Dear Matthew Fecteau, 
The NYS Department of Financial Services received a FOIL request from you on January 11, 2025. You requested: "I request all documents related to the investigation into the Tokyo Marine Group, including: 

Investigation Records: All reports, findings, and evidence related to the investigation, as well as any communications with Tokyo Marine Group regarding the violations. 

Correspondence with SIG Holdings or Safety National: All communications between DFS and SIG Holdings or Safety National related to this investigation. 

Corporate Structure: Documents detailing the corporate structure of the Tokyo Marine Group, including any by-laws, operating agreements, or other governance-related materials. 

Remediation and Compliance: Documents related to the company’s remediation plan, including corrective actions and compliance reports submitted to DFS. 

Penalties and Financial Records: Documents regarding the $720,000 civil monetary penalty, including payment records and any tax records related to the penalty." The New York State Department of Financial Services (DFS) requires additional time to respond to your request, assigned the above Reference Number, because we are still working on your request. 
DFS will endeavor to provide you the determination of your FOIL request by August 15, 2025, or sooner if possible. However, if DFS is unable to complete its review by that time, you will then be provided an update on the progress of your FOIL request. Your continued patience is greatly appreciated. 
Sincerely, Lisa Derrick 
Administrative Specialist 
NYS Department of Financial Services 
Office of General Counsel

The New York State Department of Financial Services (DFS) requires additional time to respond to your request, assigned the above Reference Number, because we are still working on your request. 
DFS will endeavor to provide you the determination of your FOIL request by October 15, 2025, or sooner if possible. However, if DFS is unable to complete its review by that time, you will then be provided an update on the progress of your FOIL request. Your continued patience is greatly appreciated. 
Sincerely, Lisa Derrick 
Administrative Specialist 
NYS Department of Financial Services 
Office of General Counsel

communications.subject: New Message :: R000576-052125
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

RE: FOIL Records Request of May 21, 2025, Reference # R000576-052125 
Dear Matthew Fecteau, 
The NYS Department of Financial Services received a FOIL request from you on May 21, 2025. You requested: "To Whom It May Concern: 
Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records: 
I am requesting access to all records in your possession from January 1, 2014, through the present concerning complaints, investigations, or regulatory actions involving Safety National Casualty Corporation, including any affiliated entities such as Safety Specialty Insurance Company and Sedgwick Claims Management Services, Inc. 
Specifically, I request copies of all consumer complaints alleging claim denials, delays, bad faith practices, misrepresentation of coverage, or failure to defend or indemnify insureds; any related correspondence or internal assessments; all regulatory enforcement actions, including market conduct examinations, administrative penalties, consent orders, fines, stipulations, or notices of violation; and any internal memoranda, emails, audit findings, or documentation regarding systemic or repeated misconduct. I also seek any statistical reports, dashboards, or complaint trend summaries reflecting activity related to Safety National during this period. 
The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 
In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 
Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 
Sincerely, 
Matthew Fecteau 
View request history, upload responsive documents, and report problems here: 
https://accounts.muckrock.com/accounts/login/?next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.muckrock.com%2Faccounts%2Flogin%2F%3Fnext%3D%252Faccounts%252Fagency_login%252Fdivision-financial-services-insurance-3603%252Fsafety-national-complaints-division-of-financial-services-insurance-186728%252F%253Femail%253DRecordsaccessofficer%252540dfs.ny.gov&url_auth_token=AAJKWcLp-HA_z-4Vyfn-Fd1oG8Y%3A1uHjgO%3AzbBwZ9s6g4IESQ1wCoMUWETv9Vc6UijkohE_YC_rDVQ 
If prompted for a passcode, please enter: 
WFWKFRVG 
Filed via MuckRock.com 
E-mail (Preferred): 186728-13968940@requests.muckrock.com 
PLEASE NOTE OUR NEW ADDRESS 
For mailed responses, please address (see note): 
MuckRock News 
DEPT MR 186728 
263 Huntington Ave 
Boston, MA 02115 
PLEASE NOTE: This request is not filed by a MuckRock staff member, but is being sent through MuckRock by the above in order to better track, share, and manage public records requests. Also note that improperly addressed (i.e., with the requester's name rather than "MuckRock News" and the department number) requests might be returned as undeliverable." 
The New York State Department of Financial Services (DFS) is still in the process of reviewing the records that you requested, and thus requires additional time to respond to your request. 
DFS will endeavor to provide you the determination of your FOIL request by October 30, 2025, or sooner if possible. However, if DFS is unable to complete its review by that time, you will then be provided an update on the progress of your FOIL request. Your continued patience is greatly appreciated. 
Sincerely, 
Carmen Garrett 
NYS Department of Financial Services

communications.subject: New Message :: R000022-011125
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

RE: FOIL Records Request of January 11, 2025, Reference # R000022-011125 
Dear Matthew Fecteau, 
The NYS Department of Financial Services received a FOIL request from you on January 11, 2025. You requested: "I request all documents related to the investigation into the Tokyo Marine Group, including: 

Investigation Records: All reports, findings, and evidence related to the investigation, as well as any communications with Tokyo Marine Group regarding the violations. 

Correspondence with SIG Holdings or Safety National: All communications between DFS and SIG Holdings or Safety National related to this investigation. 

Corporate Structure: Documents detailing the corporate structure of the Tokyo Marine Group, including any by-laws, operating agreements, or other governance-related materials. 

Remediation and Compliance: Documents related to the company’s remediation plan, including corrective actions and compliance reports submitted to DFS. 

Penalties and Financial Records: Documents regarding the $720,000 civil monetary penalty, including payment records and any tax records related to the penalty." The New York State Department of Financial Services (DFS) requires additional time to respond to your request, assigned the above Reference Number, because we are still working on your request.

communications.subject: New Message :: R000023-011125
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

RE: FOIL Records Request of January 11, 2025, Reference # R000023-011125 
Dear Matthew Fecteau, 
The NYS Department of Financial Services received a FOIL request from you on January 11, 2025. You requested: "Pursuant to applicable laws and regulations, I am requesting access to any and all records, documents, and information related to First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company that are pertinent to assessing corporate governance, intercompany relationships, and financial practices. This request covers the period from January 1, 2018, to the date this request is processed (attached is an outdated but relevant document). Specifically, I seek: 

Corporate Governance and Structure: 

Organizational charts detailing First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company’s relationship with its parent companies, including Reliance Standard Life Insurance, Delphi Financial Group, and Tokio Marine Holdings, and its affiliates. 
Board meeting minutes and resolutions involving First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company and its parent entities. 
Policies or communications governing decision-making processes, including approval of dividends or capital allocations. 
Financial Records: 

Records of intercompany loans, transfers, or payments involving First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company and its affiliates or parent companies. 
Financial statements, audits, or reports evidencing First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company’s capitalization, reserves, and financial independence. 
Documentation on dividend payments made by First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company to its parent companies, including justification and impact on surplus reserves. 
Operational Independence: 

Service agreements, cost-sharing agreements, or contracts between First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company and affiliated entities, such as claims management, IT services, and administrative support. 
Documents showing First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company’s reinsurance arrangements, including agreements with affiliated or unaffiliated reinsurers. 
Compliance and Oversight: 

Correspondence with regulatory agencies, including the New York State Department of Financial Services, regarding First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company’s compliance with insurance regulations and financial standards. 
Internal audit reports or external reviews addressing First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company’s operations, financial health, or governance. 
Shared Resources and Branding: 

Records or policies demonstrating shared resources, personnel, or branding between First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company and its parent companies or affiliates. 
Communications discussing First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company’s operational independence or its role within the broader Tokio Marine Group. 
If certain records are exempt from disclosure, please provide the non-exempt portions along with a detailed explanation of the exemptions applied. I am willing to pay reasonable fees associated with fulfilling this request, but I request notification in advance if costs exceed a reasonable amount. 

Please send responsive documents electronically to my email address if possible. I appreciate your attention to this matter and look forward to your response within the timeframe required by law." The New York State Department of Financial Services (DFS) requires additional time to respond to your request, assigned the above Reference Number, because we are still working on your request. 
DFS will endeavor to provide you the determination of your FOIL request by December 15, 2025, or sooner if possible. However, if DFS is unable to complete its review by that time, you will then be provided an update on the progress of your FOIL request. Your continued patience is greatly appreciated. 
Sincerely, Lisa Derrick 
Administrative Specialist 
NYS Department of Financial Services 
Office of General Counsel

Dear Matthew Fecteau,

I am requesting access to all records in your possession from January 1, 2014, through the present concerning complaints, investigations, or regulatory actions involving Safety National Casualty Corporation, including any affiliated entities such as Safety Specialty Insurance Company and Sedgwick Claims Management Services, Inc. 
Specifically, I request copies of all consumer complaints alleging claim denials, delays, bad faith practices, misrepresentation of coverage, or failure to defend or indemnify insureds; any related correspondence or internal assessments; all regulatory enforcement actions, including market conduct examinations, administrative penalties, consent orders, fines, stipulations, or notices of violation; and any internal memoranda, emails, audit findings, or documentation regarding systemic or repeated misconduct. Sincerely, 
Matthew Fecteau

RE: FOIL Records Request of May 21, 2025, Reference # R000576-052125 
Dear Matthew Fecteau, 
The NYS Department of Financial Services received a FOIL request from you on May 21, 2025. You requested: "To Whom It May Concern: 
Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records: 
I am requesting access to all records in your possession from January 1, 2014, through the present concerning complaints, investigations, or regulatory actions involving Safety National Casualty Corporation, including any affiliated entities such as Safety Specialty Insurance Company and Sedgwick Claims Management Services, Inc. 
Specifically, I request copies of all consumer complaints alleging claim denials, delays, bad faith practices, misrepresentation of coverage, or failure to defend or indemnify insureds; any related correspondence or internal assessments; all regulatory enforcement actions, including market conduct examinations, administrative penalties, consent orders, fines, stipulations, or notices of violation; and any internal memoranda, emails, audit findings, or documentation regarding systemic or repeated misconduct. I also seek any statistical reports, dashboards, or complaint trend summaries reflecting activity related to Safety National during this period. 
The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 
In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 
Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 
Sincerely, 
Matthew Fecteau

communications.subject: New Message :: R000023-011125
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

RE: FOIL Records Request of January 11, 2025, Reference # R000023-011125 
Dear Matthew Fecteau, 
The NYS Department of Financial Services received a FOIL request from you on January 11, 2025. You requested: "Pursuant to applicable laws and regulations, I am requesting access to any and all records, documents, and information related to First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company that are pertinent to assessing corporate governance, intercompany relationships, and financial practices. This request covers the period from January 1, 2018, to the date this request is processed (attached is an outdated but relevant document). Specifically, I seek: 

Corporate Governance and Structure: 

Organizational charts detailing First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company’s relationship with its parent companies, including Reliance Standard Life Insurance, Delphi Financial Group, and Tokio Marine Holdings, and its affiliates. 
Board meeting minutes and resolutions involving First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company and its parent entities. 
Policies or communications governing decision-making processes, including approval of dividends or capital allocations. 
Financial Records: 

Records of intercompany loans, transfers, or payments involving First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company and its affiliates or parent companies. 
Financial statements, audits, or reports evidencing First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company’s capitalization, reserves, and financial independence. 
Documentation on dividend payments made by First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company to its parent companies, including justification and impact on surplus reserves. 
Operational Independence: 

Service agreements, cost-sharing agreements, or contracts between First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company and affiliated entities, such as claims management, IT services, and administrative support. 
Documents showing First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company’s reinsurance arrangements, including agreements with affiliated or unaffiliated reinsurers. 
Compliance and Oversight: 

Correspondence with regulatory agencies, including the New York State Department of Financial Services, regarding First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company’s compliance with insurance regulations and financial standards. 
Internal audit reports or external reviews addressing First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company’s operations, financial health, or governance. 
Shared Resources and Branding: 

Records or policies demonstrating shared resources, personnel, or branding between First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company and its parent companies or affiliates. 
Communications discussing First Reliance Standard Life Insurance Company’s operational independence or its role within the broader Tokio Marine Group. 
If certain records are exempt from disclosure, please provide the non-exempt portions along with a detailed explanation of the exemptions applied. I am willing to pay reasonable fees associated with fulfilling this request, but I request notification in advance if costs exceed a reasonable amount. 

Please send responsive documents electronically to my email address if possible. I appreciate your attention to this matter and look forward to your response within the timeframe required by law." 
The New York State Department of Financial Services (DFS) is still working on your request and thus requires additional time to respond to your request. 
DFS will endeavor to provide you the determination of your FOIL request by June 09, 2026, or sooner if possible. However, if DFS is unable to complete its review by that time, you will then be provided an update on the progress of your FOIL request. Your continued patience is greatly appreciated. 
Sincerely, 
Edward MeltzerLegal Assistant 
NYS Department of Financial Services 
Office of General Counsel

title: Longitudinal Public Health Research (2016 - 2030)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Longitudinal Public Health Research (2016 - 2030)
communications.communication:
To FOIA Officer:

I am a researcher currently conducting a longitudinal study on the intersections of federal strategic planning and public health outcomes. Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, to support my analysis of the study, I am requesting the following public administrative records for the agency's last three Strategic Planning cycles.

1. Public Comment Records: Copies of written stakeholder feedback received during the formal input periods for these three Strategic Plan cycles:
• 2016 - 2020
• 2023 - 2026
• 2026 - 2030

2. Meeting/Hearing Minutes: Copies of the minutes or summary reports from any public hearings or townhall meetings held specifically to discuss the draft versions of these three Strategic Plans: 
• 2016 - 2020
• 2023 - 2026
• 2026 - 2030

3. Notice Documentation: Copies of the public notices (e.g., Federal Register Notices) used to announce the comment periods and hearings for these three Strategic Plans:
• 2016 - 2020
• 2023 - 2026
• 2026 - 2030

Fee Waiver:
I request that all fees be waived as this request is made for educational/scientific research purposes and is not for commercial use. To minimize administrative burden and costs, I am requesting all records be provided in digital format (e.g., PDF).

If the fees cannot be waived, please notify me before proceeding. I authorize up to $25 for any necessary research fees, though I anticipate these will be waived under my researcher status.

Processing & Delivery:
I am not seeking expedited processing, as this is part of a long-term comparative study. Please deliver the digital copies to the email address associated with this MuckRock request.

I look forward to your response within the statutory window of 20 business days.

Sincerely,
Ryan Miller
