Thank you for submitting a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request with the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission.  We are in receipt of the request you submitted.  Your submission has been assigned to case/request number #26-F-00308.   Please reference the request number #26-F-00308 in all future correspondence with the Commission's FOIA staff. 

Due to certain procedural steps we are required to take under our statute, there may be delays in providing the records.  Please be assured that every effort is being made to process each request as equitably as possible, and that the records or information that you have requested will be made available to you at the earliest possible date. title: Dallas April 7 th Incident (Dallas Fire-Rescue Department)

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Dallas April 7 th Incident (Dallas Fire-Rescue Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Incident Number: 26-0655127

Location: 900 Block of Pacific Avenue, Dallas, TX

Date/Time: April 7, 2026, approximately 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM

I am requesting all records related to this incident in the broadest possible scope, including but not limited to: incident reports, supplemental reports, dispatch logs, CAD records, unit activity logs, communications records, after-action reports, any records related to bomb threat assessment or response, evacuation orders or coordination records, mutual aid requests or responses from outside agencies, and any other records generated in connection with this incident or the response thereto.
If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific exemption claimed and provide a written statement of the reasons for the withholding as required by Tex. Gov't Code § 552.301.
I request a response within the timeframe required by law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Peter Herring

communications.communication:
Dear Peter  Herring: Thank you for your interest in public records of the City of Dallas. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with Chapter 552 of Texas Government Code, the Public Information Act. Your request was received in this office on 5/6/2026 and given the reference number: C006022-050626 for tracking purposes. Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern:

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific exemption claimed and provide a written statement of the reasons for the withholding as required by Tex. Gov't Code § 552.301. 
I request a response within the timeframe required by law. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Peter Herring 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2274333/, 5/7/2026". Please log in to the Records Center to retrieve the appropriate response.  The information you requested contains the following confidential information and/or information that is excepted from disclosure under section 552.021 of the Texas Government Code: [ ] The home address, home telephone number, emergency contact information, social security number, and/or family member information of a public employee or official.  As allowed by section 552.024 of the Texas Government Code, this public employee or official has chosen to make this personal information confidential.  Under section 552.024 of the Texas Government Code, our office is prohibited by law from releasing this personal information to you, and therefore we have removed this information from the enclosed information we are providing to you. [ ] Section 552.117: Confidentiality of Certain Addresses, Telephone Numbers, Social Security Numbers, and Personal Family Information. The 87th Legislature passed five different bills, Senate Bills 56, 841 and 1134 and House Bills 1082 and 3607, amending section 552.117 of the Government Code. Section 552.117 excepts from required public disclosure. (1) a current or former official or employee of a governmental body, except as otherwise provided by Section 552.024; (2)  a current or honorably retired peace officer as defined by Article 2.12, Code of Criminal Procedure, or a current or honorably retired security officer commissioned under Section 51.212, Education Code, regardless of whether the officer complies with Section 552.024 or 552 as applicable; (3) a current or former employee of the Texas Department of Criminal Justice or of the predecessor in function of the department or any division of the department, regardless of whether the current or former employee complies with Section 552.1175; [ ] A motor vehicle operator’s or driver’s license or permit issued by an agency of this state or another state or country; or a motor vehicle title or registration issued by an agency of this state or another state or country; or a personal identification document issued by an agency of this state or another state or country or a local agency authorized to issue an identification document.  This information is confidential under section 552.130 of the Texas Government Code.  Our office is prohibited by law from releasing this information to you, and therefore we have removed this information from the enclosed information we are providing to you. [ ] Litigation Exception. This information is confidential under section 552.103 of the Texas Government Code. Our office is prohibited by law from releasing this information to you. [ ] An e-mail address of a member of the public provided for the purpose of communicating electronically with a governmental body is confidential under section 552.137 of the Texas Government Code and has been removed pursuant to Attorney General Open Records Decision No. 684 (2009). [ ]  A credit card, debit card, charge card, or access device number that is collected, assembled, or maintained by or for a governmental body.  This information is confidential under section 552.136 of the Texas Government Code.  Our office is prohibited by law from releasing this information to you,   and therefore we have removed this information from the enclosed information we are providing to you. [ ] Social security numbers of living persons are excepted from the requirements of section 552.021 of the Texas Government Code.  Under section 552.147 of the Texas Government. Our office is prohibited by law from releasing this information to you, and therefore we have removed this information from the enclosed information we are providing to you. [ ]  Dates of birth are excepted from disclosure under sections 552.101 and/or 552.102 of the Texas Government Code. Pursuant to a previous determination from the Office of the Attorney General, the city has removed this information from the enclosed information we are providing to you (Open Records Letter Ruling No. 2017-09757 (2017). [ ] W-4 forms are confidential and have been removed under section 552.101 of the Texas Government Code pursuant to Attorney General Open Records Decision No. 684 (2009). [ ] Form I-9 Employment Eligibility Certification and all attachments are confidential and have been removed under section 552.101 of the Texas Government Code pursuant to Attorney General Open Records Decision No. 684 (2009). [ ] Military discharge records are confidential and have been removed under section 552.140 of the Texas Government Code pursuant to Attorney General Open Records Decision No. 684 (2009). [ ] The home address, home telephone number, emergency contact information, social security number, and family member information of certain individuals, when that information is held by a governmental body in a non-employment capacity.  As allowed by section 552.1175 of the Texas Government Code, this public employee, official, or peace officer elected to keep this personal information confidential.  Under section 552.1175 of the Texas Government Code, our office is prohibited by law from releasing this personal information to you, and therefore we have removed this information from the enclosed information we are providing to you. [ ] Direct deposit authorization forms are confidential and have been removed under section 552.101 of the Texas Government Code pursuant to Attorney General Open Records Decision No. 684 (2009). [ ] Certified agendas and tapes of closed meetings are confidential under section 552.101 of the Texas Government Code in conjunction with Section 551.104 of the Texas Government Code and have been removed pursuant to Attorney General Open Records Decision No. 684 (2009). [ ] An originating address of a 9-1-1 caller furnished to the department by a service supplier established in accordance with chapter 772 of the Health and Safety Code under section 552.101 of the Government Code in conjunction with section 772.318 of the Health and Safety Code is confidential and has been removed pursuant to a previous determination from the Office of the Attorney General (Open Records Letter Ruling No. 2011-18466 (2011)). [ ] Personal information in a customer’s account record and any information relating to the volume or units of utility usage or the amounts billed to or collected from the individual for utility usage of confidential water account customers is confidential under section 552.101 of the Texas Government Code in conjunction with section 182.052 of the Utilities Code.  Under section 552.101 of the Texas Government Code, our office is prohibited by law from releasing this information to you, and therefore we have removed this information from the enclosed information we are providing to you. [ ] Section 261.201(a) applies to a report of and information used or developed in an investigation of suspected abuse or neglect of a child and the identity of the individual who made the report of abuse or neglect.  Section 261.201(h), however, states section 261.201 does not apply to investigations of abuse or neglect in a home or facility regulated under chapter 42 of the Human Resources Code, such as a childcare facility. [ ] Section 58.008(b) applies only to the records of a child who is alleged to have engaged in delinquent conduct or conduct indicating a need for supervision. Section 58.008 applies to records created before, on, or after September 1, 2017. Section 58.008(b) does not apply where the information in question involves a juvenile as only a complainant, witness, or individual party and not a juvenile as a suspect or offender. Section 58.008(b) applies to entire law enforcement records; therefore, a law enforcement entity is generally prohibited from releasing even basic information from an investigation file when section 58.008(b) applies. [ ] Section 552.108 excepts from required public disclosure four categories of information: 1) information the release of which would interfere with the detection, investigation, or prosecution of crime or law enforcement; 2) information relating to an investigation that did not result in a conviction or deferred adjudication.  3) information relating to a threat against a peace officer or detention officer collected or disseminated under section 411.048; and 4) information that is prepared by a prosecutor in anticipation or in preparation for criminal litigation or that reflects the prosecutor’s mental impressions or legal reasoning. [ ] Section 552.101 in conjunction with article 2B.0112(f) excepts properly requested body worn camera recordings pertaining to the investigation of conduct that constitutes a misdemeanor punishable by fine only and does not result in arrest and has been removed pursuant to a previous determination from the Office of the Attorney General (Open Records Letter Ruling No. OR2026-013747 (2026)). [ ] Requestor consents to the redaction of information that is subject to the mandatory exceptions, provided such redactions are clearly labeled on the information you receive. You were not required to agree to redaction of any information. Since you agreed to redactions in this request and the city is redacting mandatory exceptions based on consent, then you may request the redacted information in a future, new request. [ ] Requestor consents to the redactions of information that is subject to discretionary exceptions, provided such redactions are clearly labeled on the information you receive. You were not required to agree to the redaction of any information. Since you agree to reactions in this request and the City is redacting discretionary exceptions based on consent, then you may request the redacted information in a future, new request. 
 you agreed to redactions in this request and the City is redacting discretionary exceptions based on consent, then you may request the redacted information in a future, new request. 
 [insert section] [ x ] Normally, we must request a ruling from the Texas Attorney General before we can withhold some of the information you requested.  However, sections 552.136 (Account Access), 552.101 (Common Law-Confidential Information), 552.102 (Confidentiality of Certain Personnel Information), 552.137 (Confidentiality of Certain E-Mail Addresses), 159.002 (Medical Records), 552.147 (Social Security), 411.090 (Criminal History), 772.218/772 (Originating telephone numbers and addresses of 9-1-1 callers), 560.065 Transportation code (Accident Reports), 552.130 (Motor Vehicle), 552.127 (Crime Watch), 552.1085 (Confidentiality of Sensitive Crime Scene Image) llow us to withhold this specific information without requesting a ruling from the attorney general. We have also received previous determinations that allow us to withhold some of the requested information pursuant to Attorney General Open Records Decision No. 684 (2009) and Open Records Letter Ruling No. 2017-09757 (2017). You have the right to appeal our decision to withhold some of the information from you. Instructions for appeal are at the end of this letter.  If you do not want to appeal, you do not need to do anything else. We will process the rest of your request for information in accordance with the terms of the Public Information Act. Respectfully, DPD - Open Records Phone: 214-671-3148 How to appeal the withholding of information under Gov’t Code sections 552.024, 552.1175, 552.130, and 552.136: If you wish to appeal the withholding of information discussed on the previous page, you must send the following to the attorney general: 1) a signed, written statement indicating your wish to appeal the withholding of information; 2) the name of the governmental body that withheld information from you; 3) the date you made your original request for information; and 4) a copy of your original request for information, or if you are unable to provide a copy, a description of your original request for information. You may also submit written comments stating why you think the information should be released to you, but you are not required to do so. Send your appeal by mail to the attorney general at: Open Records Division P.O. Box 12548 Austin, Texas 78711-2548 Within forty-five business days after receiving all of the above-listed items necessary to file your appeal, the attorney general will issue a written ruling on the matter.  You will receive a copy of this ruling in the mail.

title: Banned books list (North Carolina Department of Corrections)

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Banned books list (North Carolina Department of Corrections)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

A list of all books and other printed publications that are banned or otherwise not permitted in prisons. Please include the title and author of the publication. If available, please include the reasons for prohibiting the publication, as well as the date of the decision.

If possible, please send this information in a searchable spreadsheet format, such as an Excel file or a CSV file.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

title: Flock Safety ALPR Audits 2025 (Glenview Police Department Illinois)

communications.communication:
RE: Flock Safety ALPR Audits

To the FOIA Officer of Glenview Police Department Illinois,

This request is made under the provisions of Illinois Freedom of Information Act. for the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

Please provide the audit data in a structured format such as .csv, .xls, or another machine-readable database filetype.

3. Data Sharing
a. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares ALPR data
b. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives ALPR data
c. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information
d. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the case of any applicable fees, it is requested that an estimate of the total charges be provided prior to fulfilling this request. The preferred method of receipt is through electronic delivery.

Your assistance and cooperation in this matter are greatly appreciated. A response within 5 business days, as the statute requires is anticipated.

Best,
David S. (he/him/his), Lucy Parsons Labs

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Flock Safety ALPR Audits 2025 (Glenview Police Department Illinois)
communications.communication:
Hello, 

I am reaching out in an attempt to confer to resolve this FOIA response. I am withdrawing items one and three of my request.  Furthermore, I am limiting the scope of the search for item two of the request to all data pertaining to federal agencies or law enforcement agencies located in any of the following states:

- Alabama
- Arkansas
- Idaho
- Indiana
- Kentucky
- Louisiana
- Mississippi
- Oklahoma
- South Dakota
- Tennessee
- Texas
- West Virginia

Please confirm receipt of my narrowed FOIA request and issue a response within five business days.

Thank you.

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Flock Safety ALPR Audits 2025 (Glenview Police Department Illinois)
communications.communication:
Hello,

Glenview is claiming exemption 7(1)(d-5) but that only applies to law enforcement records contained in a shared system "if the law enforcement agency that is the recipient of the request did not create the record, did not participate in or have a role in any of the events which are the subject of the record, and only has access to the record through the shared electronic record management system."  

Glenview clearly had a role in the events that are the subject of the record because the requested data pertains to searches of Glenview's data contained in the Flock system.  Furthermore, Glenview should have possession and control of its data contained in the Flock System, which is exportable without requiring the creation of a new record.  Because it is capable of querying and exporting the data, which is in Glenview's full possession and control, Glenview is the creator of the responsive records and Exemption 7(1)(d-5) plainly does not apply.

Your assistance and cooperation in this matter are greatly appreciated.

title: Public Records Request: Flock audits

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Public Records Request: Flock audits
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

communications.subject: CPRA Request
communications.communication:
Greetings,

The Amador County Sheriff's Office received your request for records pertaining to several available audits from Flock, namely the Network Audit, Organizational Audit, and the Event Log, dating back to January of 2023.  As relatively new users of the system the Sheriff's Office only has audit records dating back to 2025.

The Organizational Audit and Network Audit are considered investigatory records and are therefore exempt from disclosure pursuant to 7923.600(a) GC.

While the Network Audit contains investigatory record information in its raw form, we were able to redact the information that is not subject to disclosure.  The remaining information has been attached in a spreadsheet.

If you have any questions you are welcome to call me at the number listed below.

Sincerely,

Jarret


Undersheriff Jarret Benov
Amador County Sheriff's Office
700 Court Street
Jackson CA 95642
209-223-6542 (Desk)
209-223-1609 (FAX)

title: Flock ALPR Contract and Audits (Central California Intelligence Center)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock ALPR Contract and Audits (Central California Intelligence Center)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please provide the following:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of November 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of November 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. A current list of network share settings that include:
3a. Flock networks shared with me
3b. Networks that I’m sharing

4. All active Master Service Agreements, contracts, NDAs and order forms with Flock Safety.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Syd Jones

title: 2015-03-05 911 Suicide Welfare Check @ 10533 Kling St. Toluca Lake, CA, 91602 (Los Angeles Police Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: 2015-03-05 911 Suicide Welfare Check @ 10533 Kling St. Toluca Lake, CA, 91602 (Los Angeles Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records relating to a welfare check (including any suicide welfare check) occurring on or about March 5th, 2015, at 10533 Kling St. Toluca Lake, CA, 91602, involving Jonathan Rees aka Greg Ellis. Incident reports; complaint reports; aided reports; case reports; narrative reports; call-for-service records; CAD records; dispatch logs; event chronologies; dispatcher notes; unit histories; 911 call recordings; non-emergency call recordings; audio recordings; transcripts; transcript excerpts; call summaries; records reflecting the reporting party’s self-identification; stated name of the reporting party; stated relationship of the reporting party to the subject; callback number; ANI (Automatic Number Identification); caller ID; telephone number associated with the caller; records showing the date and time of the call; records showing the nature of the complaint; records showing the disposition of the call; records identifying responding personnel.

If any portion of these records is exempt from disclosure, I request that all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions be provided. For any material withheld or redacted, please provide the specific statutory basis for each denial or redaction.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Pete Odin

title: Flock Safety ALPR Audits 2025 (Winnetka Police Department)

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Flock Safety ALPR Audits 2025 (Winnetka Police Department)
communications.communication:
RE: Flock Safety ALPR Audits

To the FOIA Officer of Winnetka Police Department,

This request is made under the provisions of Illinois Freedom of Information Act. for the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of June 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of June 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

Please provide the audit data in a structured format such as .csv, .xls, or another machine-readable database filetype.

3. Data Sharing
a. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares ALPR data
b. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives ALPR data
c. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information
d. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the case of any applicable fees, it is requested that an estimate of the total charges be provided prior to fulfilling this request. The preferred method of receipt is through electronic delivery.

Your assistance and cooperation in this matter are greatly appreciated. A response within 5 business days, as the statute requires is anticipated.

Best,
David S. (he/him/his), Lucy Parsons Labs

Hello,

I just spoke with Records Supervisor Kim Hodge at 6-10-2025 12:30pm and I explained it was not my intention to narrow the request but regardless that what was how it was understood. In any case, Kim was insistent that they are required to review the records regardless of the type of audit or ability to redact an entire column of private data from the records . I agreed to narrow the time frame and update the request. My amended request is:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.

Please provide the audit data in a structured format such as .csv, .xls, or another machine-readable database filetype.

3. Data Sharing

b. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives ALPR data
c. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information
d. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request #25-303
communications.communication:
Hello, 

I am reaching out in an attempt to confer to resolve this FOIA response. I am withdrawing items one and three of my request.  Furthermore, I am limiting the scope of the search for item two of the request to all data pertaining to federal agencies or law enforcement agencies located in any of the following states:

- Alabama
- Arkansas
- Idaho
- Indiana
- Kentucky
- Louisiana
- Mississippi
- Oklahoma
- South Dakota
- Tennessee
- Texas
- West Virginia

Please confirm receipt of my narrowed FOIA request and issue a response within five business days.

Thank you.

communications.subject: [External Message Added] Village of Winnetka public records request #25-303
communications.communication:
Village of Winnetka ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #25-303: You have messaged the Village of Winnetka, regarding FOIA Request #25-303 “RE: Flock Safety ALPR Audits To the FOIA Officer of Winnetka Police Department, This request is made under the provisions of Illinois Freedom of Information Act. for the following records: 1. Organization Audit in Flock. The report should include data logged from the period of June 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency. 2. Network Audit in Flock. The report should include data logged from the period of June 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System. Please provide the audit data in a structured format such as .csv, .xls, or another machine-readable database filetype. 3. Data Sharing a. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares ALPR data b. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives ALPR data c. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information d. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. In the case of any applicable fees, it is requested that an estimate of the total charges be provided prior to fulfilling this request. The preferred method of receipt is through electronic delivery. Your assistance and cooperation in this matter are greatly appreciated. A response within 5 business days, as the statute requires is anticipated. Best, David S. (he/him/his), Lucy Parsons Labs” You initially submitted this Request on May 22, 2025, seeking certain Village public records. On May 30, 2025, the Village sent you a notice inviting you to meet and confer regarding the Request. On June 6, 2025, the Village partially released records responsive to your Request and partially denied the Request. On June 10th you sent a narrowed Request and on June 18th the Village partially responded to your Request and closed the Request on June 18, 2025. On April 9, 2026, you messaged seeking to narrow this Request. Because the Request was previously responded to and closed, the Village will not further narrow the Request. However, if you wish to seek a narrower set of records, you may submit a new FOIA request describing the records you are seeking, and the Village will review and respond to that new request in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at Village of Winnetka.

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request #26-333
communications.communication:
Hello I called over and left a voicemail with Records Supervisor Kim Hodge. In the last letter sent on 4/30/2026 it was indicated that:

“The records the Village identified contain over 2 million lines of entries that are potentially responsive to your request, which would need to be individually reviewed to determine what information is exempt from disclosure. “

That’s a lot of searches on Winnetka’s Flock Safety ALPR surveillance data from states that criminalize abortion. How many lines of network searches on your data is appropriate for a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request so as to not burden the Village of Winnetka under FOIA exemption 5 ILCS140/3(g)?  This will help me better understand how to further narrow my request for accountability operator use logs of Winnetka’s surveillance system. Thanks

title: 2023 May-Sep Reports & Records Upstate NY (Chenango County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: 2023 May-Sep Reports & Records Upstate NY (Chenango County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

All records involving Jonathan Rees aka Greg Ellis from May 2023 to September 2023, including, but not limited to: incident reports; complaint reports; aided reports; case reports; narrative reports; call-for-service records; CAD records; dispatch logs; event chronologies; dispatcher notes; unit histories; 911 call recordings; non-emergency call recordings; audio recordings; transcripts; transcript excerpts; call summaries; records reflecting the reporting party’s self-identification; stated name of the reporting party; stated relationship of the reporting party to the subject; callback number; ANI (Automatic Number Identification); caller ID; telephone number associated with the caller; records showing the date and time of the call; records showing the nature of the complaint; records showing the disposition of the call; records identifying responding personnel.

If any portion of these records is exempt from disclosure, I request that all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions be provided. For any material withheld or redacted, please provide the specific statutory basis for each denial or redaction.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Pete Odin

title: Contract for Roslindale Square Transportation Plan

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Contract for Roslindale Square Transportation Plan
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Whom It May Concern:
A Request for Proposals was issued titled “Roslindale and Maverick Squares Transportation Action Plan and Design, Boston Transportation Department, Nicolos Gove EV00014797.” 
I hereby request the following records in regard to this contract, that pertain to data gathering, analysis, and community engagement conducted for Roslindale Square by the Open Box, Bowman Consulting Group, KMDG, the Boston Transportation Department and any others whose work is covered by this contract since January 1, 2024:
I -Reports and Deliverables
A. Any reports, including but not limited to:
1. Survey instruments, responses, and tabulated data summaries
2. Interim or final reports
3. Memos
4. Slide presentations
5. Findings prepared by Open Box, BTD, or other consultants related to Roslindale Square
II - Public Participation Records
A. All materials and data associated with public engagement efforts, including but not limited to:
1. Invitations, flyers, agendas, meeting minutes, slide decks, recordings, and handouts
2. Screening criteria and methods
3. Focus group interview guide
4. Individual interview guide
5. Number of individual participants and how identified
6. Number of organizational participants and how identified
7. Demographic breakdowns of participants (where collected), including but not limited to:
a. Age
b. Race/ethnicity
c. Language
d. Neighborhood
e. Whether or not a business owner, and business information including but not limited to:
i. Type of business
ii. Business address
iii. Duration of business presence in Roslindale Square
8. Survey or feedback responses categorized by:
a. Demographic information
b. Geographic information
c. Roslindale Business Owner status (yes/no)
III - Methods used to ensure equitable outreach and engagement
IV - Organizational Participation
A. Records identifying organizations that participated in public engagement or stakeholder processes, including but not limited to:
1. Organization names, number of representatives involved, and event dates
2. Role or type of participation (focus group, listening session, steering committee, etc.)
3. Contact person (name, title, email) for each participating organization (where available)
4. Any formal or informal feedback or written submissions provided by these organizations
V - Correspondence
A. Email or written correspondence between BTD staff (including Directors), Chief of Streets, Open Box, Roslindale Village Main Streets, WalkUP Roslindale, Abundant Housing, Rozzidents for More Rozzidents, including but not limited to: Anna VanRemoortel, Laura Charles, Emma Blaxter, Alyssa MacNeill, Courtney Pong, Matthew Lawlor, Benjamin Bruno, Andrew Murray, Nate Stell, Elvira Moira or others regarding Roslindale Square, particularly where engagement methods, community data, or equity analysis were discuss
Consultant Engagement and Contracts
A. All contracts, scopes of work, MOUs, RFQs, and procurement documentation relating to the engagement of Open Box or other external consultants for work on the Roslindale Square assessment
VI -Data Collection and Analysis
A. All raw and processed data collected on transportation, traffic, pedestrian movement, parking, public space use, and effects on small businesses in Roslindale Square, including but not limited to:
1. Camera footage, sensor logs, pedestrian/vehicle counts, and associated metadata
2. GIS files, maps, and visualizations (e.g., heat maps, mobility models)

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rosaline Russ

communications.subject: City Public Records Request :: R003877-072925
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
08/12/2025 RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of July 29, 2025., Reference # R003877-072925 Dear Rosaline  Russ: The City of Boston (City) has received your request for public records.  This response applies only to records that exist and are in the custody of the City. See A Guide to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, p. 32, n.115.  It is expected that a custodian of records must use her superior knowledge of her records with respect to responses to public records requests.  950 CMR 32.04(5).  Specifically, you stated: 
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records: 

Whom It May Concern: 
A Request for Proposals was issued titled “Roslindale and Maverick Squares Transportation Action Plan and Design, Boston Transportation Department, Nicolos Gove EV00014797.” 
I hereby request the following records in regard to this contract, that pertain to data gathering, analysis, and community engagement conducted for Roslindale Square by the Open Box, Bowman Consulting Group, KMDG, the Boston Transportation Department and any others whose work is covered by this contract since January 1, 2024: 
I -Reports and Deliverables 
A. Any reports, including but not limited to: 
1. Survey instruments, responses, and tabulated data summaries 
2. Interim or final reports 
3. Memos 
4. Slide presentations 
5. Findings prepared by Open Box, BTD, or other consultants related to Roslindale Square 
II - Public Participation Records 
A. All materials and data associated with public engagement efforts, including but not limited to: 
1. Invitations, flyers, agendas, meeting minutes, slide decks, recordings, and handouts 
2. Screening criteria and methods 
3. Focus group interview guide 
4. Individual interview guide 
5. Number of individual participants and how identified 
6. Number of organizational participants and how identified 
7. Demographic breakdowns of participants (where collected), including but not limited to: 
a. Age 
b. Race/ethnicity 
c. Language 
d. Neighborhood 
e. Whether or not a business owner, and business information including but not limited to: 
i. Type of business 
ii. Business address 
iii. Duration of business presence in Roslindale Square 
8. Survey or feedback responses categorized by: 
a. Demographic information 
b. Geographic information 
c. Roslindale Business Owner status (yes/no) 
III - Methods used to ensure equitable outreach and engagement 
IV - Organizational Participation 
A. Records identifying organizations that participated in public engagement or stakeholder processes, including but not limited to: 
1. Organization names, number of representatives involved, and event dates 
2. Role or type of participation (focus group, listening session, steering committee, etc.) 
3. Contact person (name, title, email) for each participating organization (where available) 
4. Any formal or informal feedback or written submissions provided by these organizations 
V - Correspondence 
A. Email or written correspondence between BTD staff (including Directors), Chief of Streets, Open Box, Roslindale Village Main Streets, WalkUP Roslindale, Abundant Housing, Rozzidents for More Rozzidents, including but not limited to: Anna VanRemoortel, Laura Charles, Emma Blaxter, Alyssa MacNeill, Courtney Pong, Matthew Lawlor, Benjamin Bruno, Andrew Murray, Nate Stell, Elvira Moira or others regarding Roslindale Square, particularly where engagement methods, community data, or equity analysis were discuss 
Consultant Engagement and Contracts 
A. All contracts, scopes of work, MOUs, RFQs, and procurement documentation relating to the engagement of Open Box or other external consultants for work on the Roslindale Square assessment 
VI -Data Collection and Analysis 
A. All raw and processed data collected on transportation, traffic, pedestrian movement, parking, public space use, and effects on small businesses in Roslindale Square, including but not limited to: 
1. Camera footage, sensor logs, pedestrian/vehicle counts, and associated metadata 
2. GIS files, maps, and visualizations (e.g., heat maps, mobility models) 

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage. 

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rosaline Russ 

Upload documents directly: https://accounts.muckrock.com/accounts/login/?next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.muckrock.com%2Faccounts%2Flogin%2F%3Fnext%3D%252Faccounts%252Fagency_login%252Fboston-department-of-procurement-42100%252Fcontract-for-roslindale-square-transportation-plan-190286%252F%253Femail%253Dprocurement%252540boston.gov&url_auth_token=AAJadgHYt2Lv3uW3vy6-_1WkgPU%3A1ugeDI%3A5yP-3oDo5MjMgiqd3e3sdv7yEfB_J4zZeQAAaZvizcQ A response to a public records request must be provided within ten (10) business days of receiving a written request.  G. L. c. 66, § 10 (a); 950 CMR 32.06(2)(b).  The public records law permits a response time of up to twenty-five (25) business days from the business day a written request is received, so long as a detailed explanation is provided.  G. L. c. 66, § 10(b)(vi); 950 CMR 32.06(2)(i). Due to the nature of your request, additional time is needed.  As such, the City will need up to, but no more than, fifteen (15) additional business days to review and process your request. Yours truly,    
  
  
 Ahir Verma 
 City Records Co-op 
 Boston City Hall | Boston, MA 02201 
 617-635-4037

Hello, 
On 9/10, a member of our staff reached out to you to indicate that your request is currently overly broad, and that we would require a narrowed scope and specific email addresses in order to fulfill your request. We asked that you respond with clarifications by 9/24, but we have not received clarification from you thus far. If you would like, the Public Records team would be happy to assist you in narrowing your request. 
The following is copied from the email you received on 9/10: 
A public records request must reasonably describe the records sought. G. L. c. 66, § 10(a)(i). In reviewing your request, we determined that your request is currently overly broad and vague and we would have to perform an unreasonable search to fulfill your request. A preliminary search in city databases yielded an overwhelming number of results. 
Furthermore, without any email addresses, Public Records would not be able to perform a narrowed search. 
In order for the City to fulfill your request, we ask that you narrow the scope of what you are seeking by September 24, 2025. The Public Records team would be happy to help narrow your search.  Requesters typically narrow their request by identifying a more specific date range or a more specific type of record. You may respond to this message on GovQA, email publicrecords@boston.gov, or call us at 617-635-4037 to discuss further. 
Best,    
  
  
 Ahir Verma

communications.subject: RE: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request #R003877-072925
communications.communication:
Good Afternoon,

I believe there may be a mix-up regarding my request. I originally filed my request to the Boston Transportation Department. After waiting awhile, I was advised to file with the Department of Procurement. 

At present and moving forward, I am pursuing this matter via the Boston Transportation Department as the BTD received the decision from the Secretary of the Commonwealth.

 On September 16, 2025, I sent an email to the Procurement Department notifying you of this matter, as detailed below.

From my September 16, 2025 email:
Good morning,
Please see the attached letter from the Secretary of the Commonwealth regarding a similar request to the Boston Transportation Department. They state:

"Accordingly, the City is ordered to provide Ms. Russ with a response to her request, provided in a manner consistent with this order, the Public Records Law and its Regulations within ten (10) business days. A copy of any such response must be provided to this office. It is preferable to send an electronic copy of the response to this office at pre@sec.state.ma.us
. Ms. Russ may appeal the substantive nature of the City’s supplemental response within ninety (90) days. See 950 C.M.R. 32.08(1)."

I look forward to receiving the requested documents.

Yours,
Rosaline Russ

 I would appreciate your acknowledgement that this request has been properly forwarded to the Boston Transportation Department. Could you please confirm that you are looking to the Boston Transportation Department to fulfill my request? The contact person for the BTD is Nia Platt *(she/her), Public Records Co-Op, Boston City Hall | Boston, MA 02201 617-635-3268.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Yours sincerely,

Rosaline Russ

communications.subject: RE: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request #R003877-072925
communications.communication:
Subject: Clarification Regarding Request R003034-061225

Good afternoon. Thank you for acknowledging that this request remains under the purview of the Boston Transportation Department, reference number R003034-061225.

As noted, the Secretary of the Commonwealth addressed their decision to the Transportation Department under this reference number, and the decision made no mention of any modification or redirection of the request. Accordingly, I look forward to the Boston Transportation Department’s continued handling of this matter in accordance with that decision.

At this point, I would also appreciate it if all further communications regarding this request come directly from the Transportation Department, rather than from the Procurement Department.

Sincerely,
Rosaline Russ

communications.subject: City Public Records Request :: R003877-072925
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
05/07/2026 RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of July 29, 2025, Reference # R003877-072925 Dear Rosaline  Russ: The City of Boston (City) has received your request for public records.  This response applies only to records that exist and are in the custody of the City. See A Guide to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, p. 32, n.115.  It is expected that a custodian of records must use her superior knowledge of her records concerning responses to public records requests.  950 CMR 32.04(5).  Specifically, you stated: 
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records: 

Whom It May Concern: 
A Request for Proposals was issued titled “Roslindale and Maverick Squares Transportation Action Plan and Design, Boston Transportation Department, Nicolos Gove EV00014797.” 
I hereby request the following records in regard to this contract, that pertain to data gathering, analysis, and community engagement conducted for Roslindale Square by the Open Box, Bowman Consulting Group, KMDG, the Boston Transportation Department and any others whose work is covered by this contract since January 1, 2024: 
I -Reports and Deliverables 
A. Any reports, including but not limited to: 
1. Survey instruments, responses, and tabulated data summaries 
2. Interim or final reports 
3. Memos 
4. Slide presentations 
5. Findings prepared by Open Box, BTD, or other consultants related to Roslindale Square 
II - Public Participation Records 
A. All materials and data associated with public engagement efforts, including but not limited to: 
1. Invitations, flyers, agendas, meeting minutes, slide decks, recordings, and handouts 
2. Screening criteria and methods 
3. Focus group interview guide 
4. Individual interview guide 
5. Number of individual participants and how identified 
6. Number of organizational participants and how identified 
7. Demographic breakdowns of participants (where collected), including but not limited to: 
a. Age 
b. Race/ethnicity 
c. Language 
d. Neighborhood 
e. Whether or not a business owner, and business information including but not limited to: 
i. Type of business 
ii. Business address 
iii. Duration of business presence in Roslindale Square 
8. Survey or feedback responses categorized by: 
a. Demographic information 
b. Geographic information 
c. Roslindale Business Owner status (yes/no) 
III - Methods used to ensure equitable outreach and engagement 
IV - Organizational Participation 
A. Records identifying organizations that participated in public engagement or stakeholder processes, including but not limited to: 
1. Organization names, number of representatives involved, and event dates 
2. Role or type of participation (focus group, listening session, steering committee, etc.) 
3. Contact person (name, title, email) for each participating organization (where available) 
4. Any formal or informal feedback or written submissions provided by these organizations 
V - Correspondence 
A. Email or written correspondence between BTD staff (including Directors), Chief of Streets, Open Box, Roslindale Village Main Streets, WalkUP Roslindale, Abundant Housing, Rozzidents for More Rozzidents, including but not limited to: Anna VanRemoortel, Laura Charles, Emma Blaxter, Alyssa MacNeill, Courtney Pong, Matthew Lawlor, Benjamin Bruno, Andrew Murray, Nate Stell, Elvira Moira or others regarding Roslindale Square, particularly where engagement methods, community data, or equity analysis were discuss 
Consultant Engagement and Contracts 
A. All contracts, scopes of work, MOUs, RFQs, and procurement documentation relating to the engagement of Open Box or other external consultants for work on the Roslindale Square assessment 
VI -Data Collection and Analysis 
A. All raw and processed data collected on transportation, traffic, pedestrian movement, parking, public space use, and effects on small businesses in Roslindale Square, including but not limited to: 
1. Camera footage, sensor logs, pedestrian/vehicle counts, and associated metadata 
2. GIS files, maps, and visualizations (e.g., heat maps, mobility models) 

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage. 

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rosaline Russ 

Upload documents directly: https://accounts.muckrock.com/accounts/login/?next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.muckrock.com%2Faccounts%2Flogin%2F%3Fnext%3D%252Faccounts%252Fagency_login%252Fboston-department-of-procurement-42100%252Fcontract-for-roslindale-square-transportation-plan-190286%252F%253Femail%253Dprocurement%252540boston.gov&url_auth_token=AAJadgHYt2Lv3uW3vy6-_1WkgPU%3A1ugeDI%3A5yP-3oDo5MjMgiqd3e3sdv7yEfB_J4zZeQAAaZvizcQ The record is redacted to omit identifying information, including phone numbers, email addresses, vendor IDs, contract IDs, insurance policy numbers, and NAIC numbers, in accordance with exemption (c) of Massachusetts Public Records law. The public interest in disclosure of this information does not outweigh the privacy interest of the individual. G. L. c. 4, § 7 (26) (c). 
Further, the record is redacted to omit information in accordance with exemption (a). Exemption a, the statutory exemption, permits the withholding of records that are specifically or by necessary implication exempted from disclosure by statute. G. L. c. 4, § 7(26)(a). Here, the records have been withheld in accordance with the Criminal Offender Record Information (CORI) Act. G. L. c. 6, § 167. 
You may appeal this response to the Supervisor of Records in the Office of the Secretary of the Commonwealth. G.L. c. 66, § 10A(a); 950 C.M.R. 32.08. You may also appeal to the Superior Court. G.L. c. 66, § 10A(c). Sincerely,    
  
 
 
  
 Marli Mason she/her 
 City Records Co-Op 
 Boston City Hall | Boston, MA 02201 
 617-635-4037

(https://u8387778.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.VEsWrlMiJDg0mXafq1mEXTIDeIcW7QPGcxbg2r6mBl4IQAtcuUR-2B6-2B48u6MWdi-2FuNfsoFtzUO-2FiE2Z5NHdOxR4-2FmG6oB7htEY3HEwbHCt9I-3DGuRm_zEBK1zRbdu57BP9MnZ-2BzljLdAb-2FP6oRKZNPV7USPcVHqLe8y-2BI7f8t-2F6-2FrWjFpI-2FL-2F-2FJ5sTtMa6YT6AS1llrYOW8EIAYk7VzaarwfI9iJxEu9mgjCAc-2BUhsetavyu2tJmEJ9LwzlDAlN1FdURr1BrsUjEfAnvc-2Fnzhftm3oM9Z1Fe-2F46arhuTymzkuTe3kIR9OdGlrcGcs-2BUoA9Rmc62NPsEMLne1aNRk6ywyblwl4nOKI0ZFg-2F3qfo5VxIy7do1TJkGbLvwtpfL3CaRTUlEX5fBIfM89Y2jFPrucBsHV3YhgGf2acAQ-2BnxkHeFlJdtuRwhT-2F05gebstZATQ0zSxDdQZLtN8MJK4t-2FnEISJIRx4b5QOrmXEv4tjBTOolzqWG)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All attachments, documents, files, or enclosures transmitted by Kerry McCormack (kerry.mccormack@flocksafety.com) of Flock Safety to Sharon Lowery and/or any City of Dunwoody official, employee, or representative, on or around February 18, 2026, referenced in Mr. McCormack's email dated February 18, 2026 at approximately 9:46 PM, in which he stated:

"Please find two informational pieces to distribute to the Mayor and Council prior to the meeting on Monday."

This request specifically includes but is not limited to:

Both "informational pieces" referenced in Mr. McCormack's February 18, 2026 email
Any presentation materials subsequently sent by Mr. McCormack to Ms. Lowery, or forwarded by Ms. Lowery, to the Mayor and members of the Dunwoody City Council in connection with the February 23, 2026 City Council meeting
Any materials forwarded by Ms. Lowery to the Mayor and/or City Council members in response to Mr. McCormack's request

Relevant timeframe: February 1, 2026 through February 28, 2026
Relevant parties: Kerry McCormack (Flock Safety), Sharon Lowery, and any Mayor or Council member who received forwarded materials
I request these records in electronic format (PDF or native file format). If any portion of this request is denied, please provide a written explanation citing the specific statutory exemption(s) relied upon for each withheld document.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

1. Provide a list or records regarding all grievance settlements including settlement amounts, union, date grievance filed, school (if applicable), date grievance settled, and contract issues of grievance from January 1, 2026 through March 31, 2026.

2. Provide a list or records regarding all grievances in arbitration including union, date grievance filed, date arbitration started, school (if applicable) and contract issues of grievance from January 1, 2026 through March 31, 2026.

3. Provide a total list of all grievances filed including union, date grievance filed, school (if applicable) and contract issues of grievance from January 1, 2026 through March 31, 2026.

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Miriam Bhimani

Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center.

communications.subject: Non-Commercial FOIA Request :: N019407-040926
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
May 7, 2026 Miriam Bhimani RE: Chicago Public Schools FOIA Request N019407-040926 Dear Miriam Bhimani: This letter is in reply to your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request seeking the following information: 1. Provide a list or records regarding all grievance settlements including settlement amounts, union, date grievance filed, school (if applicable), date grievance settled, and contract issues of grievance from January 1, 2026 through March 31, 2026. 

2. Provide a list or records regarding all grievances in arbitration including union, date grievance filed, date arbitration started, school (if applicable) and contract issues of grievance from January 1, 2026 through March 31, 2026. 

3. Provide a total list of all grievances filed including union, date grievance filed, school (if applicable) and contract issues of grievance from January 1, 2026 through March 31, 2026. RESPONSE : Documents responsive to your request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the records. The records provided include all grievances received by the Office of Administrative Hearings only. As to the remainder of all other potentially responsive data, the CPS FOIA office has determined that it is categorical in nature as it does not reasonably identify a document for search, and the collection of this information would require the District to create records. Specifically, the CPS does not comprehensively track the requested information in the manner requested, in the normal course of business. As such fulfilling this request would be considered creating records which is not required pursuant to the FOIA. Specifically, in order to comply with your request, each and every file would have to be pulled and all the portions of information you have requested would have to be searched for. Essentially, a new record would have to be created. This process is not required according to guidance provided by 5 ILCS 140/2(c). 5 ILCS 140/1, Kenyon v. Garrels, 184 Ill. App. 3d 28, 32 (4 th Dist. 1989), and Yeager v. Drug Enforcement Admin., 678 F. 2d 315, 321 (D.C. Cir. 1982). Additionally, under Illinois FOIA law, a request to inspect or copy a record must reasonably identify a public record, as opposed to general data, information or statistics to be gleaned from documents which have not otherwise been created by a public body. Accordingly, FOIA applies to the provision of existing public records; a public body is not required to answer questions or generate new records in response to a FOIA request. Further, Section 1 of the FOIA states in part: "This Act is not intended to create an obligation on the part of any public body to maintain or prepare any public record which was not maintained or prepared by such public body at the time when this Act becomes effective, except as otherwise required by applicable local, State or federal law." In accordance with Section 1 of Illinois FOIA, the District is not required to conduct research to answer questions or prepare researched records in response to a specific query [5 ILCS 140/1]. To the extent you consider this a denial, pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a) you may file a Request for Review with the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at: Public Access Counselor; Office of the Attorney General; 500 South 2nd Street; Springfield, IL 62706. You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court, 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. 
Thank you for your request. Sincerely,
